briefs.title: Port Moody Council to Recite Territorial Land Acknowledgement at April 28 Meeting
briefs.summary: Port Moody City Council will open its April 28, 2026 meeting with a Territorial Land Acknowledgement — a formal statement recognizing that the city operates on the traditional and unceded (never formally surrendered) lands of eight Indigenous Peoples, including the Kwikwetlem, Tsleil-Waututh, Musqueam, and Squamish Nations.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Port Moody Agenda Adoption — April 28, 2026
briefs.summary: Port Moody City Council is set to vote on formally adopting the agenda for its April 28, 2026 meeting. This is a procedural step — it confirms which items Council will officially discuss and decide on that day. No specific business regulations or bylaws are described in this item.

briefs.title: Council to consider: Port Moody adopts April 28, 2026 regular meeting agenda
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council is set to vote on formally adopting the agenda for its Regular Council meeting on April 28, 2026. This is a routine procedural step — it confirms which items will be discussed and decided at that meeting. No business decisions have been made yet.
briefs.action_required: Check the full April 28, 2026 Port Moody Council agenda for items that may affect your business.

key_dates.label: Port Moody Regular Council meeting — agenda adoption vote

briefs.title: Council to Consider General Matters — Port Moody Meeting April 28, 2026
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council is set to consider a range of general matters at its upcoming April 28, 2026 meeting. No specific agenda details were provided, so business owners in the area should monitor the full agenda for items that could affect local business operations, bylaws, or permits.
briefs.action_required: Check the full Port Moody Council agenda for April 28, 2026 to spot items affecting your business.

key_dates.label: Port Moody Council general matters meeting date

briefs.title: Council to Observe National Day of Mourning for Workplace Injury and Death
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council will hold a moment of silence on April 28 to mark the National Day of Mourning — a day observed across Canada each year to honour workers who have died, been injured, or fallen ill on the job. It is also a time to renew the shared commitment to making workplaces safer and preventing future tragedies.
briefs.action_required: Take a moment on April 28 to recognize the National Day of Mourning with your team.

key_dates.label: National Day of Mourning — workplace remembrance observed across Canada

briefs.industries: Labour & Employment, Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to consider verbal update on Port Moody Arts, Culture, and Heritage Committee
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council will hear a verbal report from Councillor Kyla Knowles, Chair of the Arts, Culture, and Heritage Committee, at the April 28, 2026 meeting. No specific bylaw or business regulation is proposed — this is an informational update on arts and culture matters in the city.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Battered Women's Support Services Presents #DesignedWithSurvivors Campaign
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council is set to hear a delegation from Battered Women's Support Services (BWSS) about their #DesignedWithSurvivors campaign. BWSS is asking the city to consider four steps to address gender-based violence: forming a municipal task force, standardizing risk assessment tools for first responders, appointing a dedicated staff lead, and launching a local public awareness campaign.
briefs.action_required: Watch for Council's response — this may lead to new municipal programs or policies affecting community safety.

briefs.title: Council to Consider Public Input at Port Moody Meeting — April 28, 2026
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council will open the floor to public input at its upcoming meeting on April 28, 2026. Members of the public can speak for up to two minutes or submit written comments, but must follow the city's respectful conduct policies — meaning comments should focus on issues, not individuals.
briefs.action_required: Attend the April 28 Port Moody Council meeting to speak or submit written comments on agenda items.

key_dates.label: Port Moody Council public input session — comment deadline

briefs.title: Council to consider approving minutes from Port Moody's April 14, 2026 closed-door meetings
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council is set to vote on adopting the official minutes from two April 14, 2026 meetings: a closed-door Special Council session and a Regular Council meeting. The closed session covered confidential topics including legal advice, land deals, employee matters, and negotiations with other governments — none of which are disclosed to the public.

briefs.title: Council to Consider Consent Agenda Items at Port Moody Meeting — April 28, 2026
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council is set to vote on a group of items bundled together on the Consent Agenda at its April 28, 2026 meeting. A consent agenda is a package of routine items approved all at once in a single vote — though any councillor can pull an item out for separate debate before the vote takes place.
briefs.action_required: Check the full Port Moody Council agenda for April 28 to see if any items affect your business.

key_dates.label: Port Moody Council Consent Agenda vote — April 28, 2026 meeting

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Port Moody's Response to Maple Ridge Industrial Land Expansion Proposal
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council is set to vote on whether to tell Metro Vancouver that the City has no objections to a proposed regional land use change requested by the City of Maple Ridge. The proposal would redesignate roughly 301 hectares near North 256 Street from Rural and Industrial to Industrial and Conservation and Recreation, and expand the Urban Containment Boundary (the official line that separates urban development areas from rural land) to support a new light industrial hub.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the council decision — if you operate in light industrial, logistics, or related sectors, this could signal new business land opportunities.

key_dates.label: Port Moody Council vote — Metro 2050 Maple Ridge industrial lands amendment response

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Natural Resources, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Port Moody Council to vote on opposing Surrey's Hazelmere urban expansion proposal
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council is set to vote on telling Metro Vancouver that it does not support the City of Surrey's request to reclassify roughly 24 hectares of rural land near the U.S. border as urban — a change that would allow 145 single-family lots to be built in an area far from transit and city services. Staff recommend opposing the plan because it would create 'leapfrog' development (urban growth that skips over rural land, disconnected from existing neighbourhoods), strain the regional sewer system, and conflict with Metro Vancouver's regional growth strategy, Metro 2050.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the Port Moody Council decision on April 28, 2026 — it could influence Metro Vancouver's final ruling on this Surrey land-use change.

key_dates.label: Port Moody Council vote — Metro 2050 Surrey Hazelmere amendment response deadline

briefs.title: Council to Consider Approving New School Sites Plan for Port Moody and SD43
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council is set to vote on accepting School District No. 43's 2027–2028 Eligible School Sites Proposal — a plan that identifies where new schools will be needed as the region grows. The proposal projects up to 7,005 new homes in Port Moody over the next 10 years, with one new school site planned for the Port Moody Centre waterfront area to serve future residents.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — new school sites can affect where future development is approved nearby.

key_dates.label: Port Moody Council vote — SD43 2027-2028 Eligible School Sites Proposal

briefs.title: Proposed: Port Moody to raise Highway Use Permit fees and update pay parking rates
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council is set to vote on a bylaw amendment that would increase fees for Highway Use Permits (HUPs) — permits required when businesses or contractors occupy city roads or sidewalks for construction or other work — and update pay parking rates. The biggest change: weekly fees for permits on major and local roads would rise sharply after the first week, pushing long-term permit holders to wrap up work faster.
briefs.action_required: If your business holds or plans to apply for a Highway Use Permit, review the new weekly fee schedule before April 28, 2026.

key_dates.label: Port Moody HUP and pay parking fee bylaw — Council vote date

briefs.title: Council to Consider Port Moody's Proposed 2026–2030 Five-Year Financial Plan
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council is set to review a proposed Five-Year Financial Plan covering 2026 to 2030, presented by the General Manager of Finance and Technology. This plan outlines how the city expects to spend and raise money over the next five years — including taxes, fees, and services — and has not yet been approved.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — approved spending plans can affect local taxes and city fees for your business.

key_dates.label: Port Moody 2026–2030 Five-Year Financial Plan — council consideration date

briefs.title: Proposed: Port Moody Council to vote on 2026–2030 Five-Year Financial Plan Bylaw
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council is set to vote on Bylaw No. 3540, which would set the city's budget for 2026 through 2030. If approved, the bylaw would mean a roughly 3.95% property tax increase for the average home (assessed at $1.22M) — about $121 more per year — plus a total blended property charge (taxes and utility fees combined) of around $3,316, up 4.00% from 2025.
briefs.action_required: Check your Port Moody property tax and utility bill — a ~4% increase is proposed for 2026.

key_dates.label: Port Moody Financial Plan Bylaw No. 3540 — council vote (three readings)
Port Moody Financial Plan and Tax Rates Bylaw — adoption deadline under Community Charter

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to vote on Port Moody's 2026 property tax rates bylaw
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council is set to vote on Bylaw No. 3541, which would set the city's property tax rates for 2026. If approved, the average homeowner (with a home assessed at $1.22 million) would pay about $3,179 in municipal property tax — a $121 or roughly 3.95% increase over 2025, working out to about $2.32 more per week.
briefs.action_required: Check your 2026 property tax bill — the due date is July 2, 2026, with a 5% penalty starting July 3.

key_dates.label: Port Moody 2026 property tax rates bylaw — must be adopted by

briefs.title: Council to vote on Port Moody 2026 property tax penalty schedule and due dates
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council is set to vote on Bylaw No. 3542, which would set the rules for when penalties are added to unpaid property taxes in 2026. Under the proposed bylaw, a 5% penalty would apply to unpaid taxes after July 2, 2026, and a second 5% penalty would be added for any payments still outstanding after September 15, 2026.
briefs.action_required: Pay your Port Moody property taxes by July 2, 2026 to avoid a 5% penalty.

key_dates.label: Port Moody property tax first due date — 5% penalty applies if unpaid

briefs.title: Council to vote on adopting Port Moody's new Official Community Plan (2050)
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council is set to vote on adopting a new Official Community Plan (OCP) — a long-range blueprint that guides how land in the city is used, developed, and zoned over the coming decades. If approved, the new plan (Bylaw No. 3522, known as 'Port Moody 2050') would replace the current OCP and set the rules that shape future development, housing, and business decisions across the city.
briefs.action_required: Review the draft Port Moody 2050 OCP to see how land-use changes may affect your business location or plans.

key_dates.label: Port Moody Official Community Plan Bylaw No. 3522 — adoption vote (Council meeting)

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Council to consider delaying Port Moody's Community Satisfaction Survey to 2027
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council is set to vote on postponing its 2026 Community Satisfaction Survey to early 2027. Staff are recommending this change to shift the every-two-year survey schedule so it falls in years one and three of each Council term — avoiding election years and giving Council fresher data when setting priorities. The survey costs about $35,000 and would continue to be run online by an independent research company.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — no direct business impact expected from this administrative change.

key_dates.label: Port Moody pre-election quiet period begins — LECFA rules take effect

briefs.title: Council to Consider $3,200 Funding Request from Inlet Music Series for Theatre Costs
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council will consider whether to fund the Inlet Music Series up to $3,200 per year for two years to help cover theatre rental fees at the Inlet Theatre. The volunteer-run group has put on concerts there for four years and is asking the City to also continue promoting their events through newsletters, digital signs, and posters.
briefs.action_required: If you support or are affected by local arts funding, watch for the Council decision at the April 28, 2026 meeting.

key_dates.label: Port Moody Council meeting — Inlet Music Series funding decision

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Port Moody 2050 Official Community Plan Update and Land Use Changes
briefs.summary: Port Moody's Land Use Committee has reviewed a draft update to the city's Official Community Plan (OCP) — a long-term blueprint that sets the rules for how land can be used and developed across the city. The draft plan is set to go to Council for consideration, and covers key issues like housing density near transit, protecting industrial land, and managing growth in special study areas.
briefs.action_required: Review the draft Port Moody 2050 OCP on the Engage Port Moody website and submit your feedback before Council votes.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to consider adding MPS Day and World MS Day lighting to Port Moody schedule
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council is set to vote on adding two special lighting dates to the city's official lighting schedule: purple lights on May 15, 2026 for International MPS Day, and red lights on May 30, 2026 for World MS Day. These are awareness-based lighting requests and are not yet approved.

briefs.title: Proposed: Metro Vancouver Rolls Back Development Cost Charges for 2026 and 2027
briefs.summary: Metro Vancouver's regional boards have given initial approval to bylaws that would roll back Development Cost Charges (DCCs — one-time fees developers pay to fund new infrastructure) to 2025 levels for 2026, and reduce planned increases for 2027. These changes are still awaiting final approval from the provincial Inspector of Municipalities before they take effect.
briefs.action_required: If you are in construction or development, monitor the Inspector of Municipalities' decision on the DCC bylaw changes.

key_dates.label: Metro Vancouver DCC amendment bylaws — Inspector of Municipalities approval request deadline

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Public Input Session at Port Moody Council Meeting
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council will hold a public input session where residents and business owners can speak directly to Council. Participants are expected to follow the City's Respectful Communications Policy — meaning comments should focus on issues, not individuals, and all interaction must remain respectful and constructive.
briefs.action_required: Visit the Council Meetings page to find out how to register and participate in the public input session.
